message:
fix(pager): WAL group commit flusher retry loop for lock contention

D1-CRITICAL: Resolve BusySnapshot errors in persistent WAL benchmark
(16-thread concurrent writes) by adding exponential backoff retry loop
for transient EXCLUSIVE lock contention.

Problem:
- WAL group commit flusher acquires EXCLUSIVE lock to prevent race with
  checkpoint (checkpoint can start while flusher is mid-append)
- Under high concurrency, this lock acquisition can fail with Busy
- Original implementation had no retry, causing immediate failure

Solution:
- Add MAX_FLUSH_RETRIES=10 with exponential backoff (1ms, 2ms, ... 512ms)
- Add jitter to prevent thundering herd
- Track busy_retries in ConsolidationMetrics for observability
- Downgrade to RESERVED after write completes to minimize lock hold time

Key changes:
- pager.rs: Wrap flush closure in retry loop with backoff
- group_commit.rs: Add busy_retries metric and record_busy_retry()

Benchmark results (16-thread persistent WAL):
- FrankenSQLite: 11.3-15.9s, 1.0-1.4 Kelem/s
- C SQLite: 7.5-9.0s, 1.8-2.1 Kelem/s
- No panics, all 2t/4t/8t/16t configurations pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
